2605.23165.title: Autonomous Frontier-Based Exploration with VLM Guidance
2605.23165.paper_summary: Autonomous robotic exploration of unknown and hazardous environments, a long-standing challenge, can be significantly improved by leveraging the advanced reasoning of Vision-Language Models (VLMs). We introduce a novel exploration pipeline where a VLM performs high-level strategic decision-making, guiding a conventional low-level robotics control stack. At decision points, the robot generates a multimodal prompt with its current map and visual imagery of potential paths, or frontiers. The VLM analyzes this prompt to select the most promising frontier, replacing simple geometric heuristics with contextual spatial reasoning. This approach, validated in simulation across six indoor environments, improves map coverage by up to 24\% over existing methods. Our pipeline is lightweight, training-free, and easily transferable to any robot with standard sensors and an internet connection.
2605.23165.paper_summary_zh: 自主機器人探索未知和危險環境的挑戰由來已久，通過利用視覺-語言模型（VLMs）的先進推理，可以顯著改善這一過程。<lb/>我們介紹了一種新穎的探索管道，其中VLM執行高層次的戰略決策，指導傳統的低層次機器人控制堆疊。<lb/>在決策點，機器人生成一個多模態提示，包含其當前地圖和潛在路徑或邊界的視覺影像。<lb/>VLM分析這個提示，以選擇最有前景的邊界，並用上下文空間推理取代簡單的幾何啟發式方法。<lb/>這種方法在六個室內環境的模擬中得到了驗證，地圖覆蓋率比現有方法提高了多達24%。<lb/>我們的管道輕量、無需訓練，並且可以輕鬆轉移到任何具有標準傳感器和網絡連接的機器人上。
2605.23165.author: Aarush Aitha et.al.
